steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank appears in an attribution that combines a professional descriptor with a person's name: “claims dendrochronologist _____ Valerie Trouet.”

steps.title: Apply the descriptor-name rule
steps.content: When a professional descriptor immediately precedes a person's name in an attribution, no punctuation separates them—like “claims expert John” or “tells scientist Maria.” Options A, B, and C all insert separating punctuation that would incorrectly break the descriptor from the name it identifies. The descriptor and name must form a single unit: “dendrochronologist Valerie Trouet.” That matches choice D — D is correct.
